title: Historical Navigation as QA Integer Arithmetic — 5 civilizations
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that five independent historical navigation systems used integer-ratio methods structurally equivalent to QA: Babylonian direction tables, Egyptian slope ratios, Polynesian discrete bearings, Norse spread measurements, Arab integer altitude increments. Each system operates on discrete states with observer projection at measurement boundaries — Theorem NT before the name existed.

systems.name: Babylonian (Plimpton 322)
systems.method: Sexagesimal Pythagorean triple table. Each row gives a direction (d,e) and derived values C=2de, F=d²-e², G=d²+e². Organized by decreasing δ/α ratio (spread-like ordering).
systems.qa_equivalent: Berggren tree nodes. Each row = gnomonic chart waypoint. G = direction quadrance. The tablet IS a QA navigation table (cert [138]).
systems._note: All 15 rows of Plimpton 322 are Pythagorean triples. Mansfield & Wildberger (2017) proved this is exact trigonometry, not a pedagogical exercise.

systems.name: Egyptian Seked
systems.method: Seked = horizontal displacement per 1 cubit of vertical rise. Measured in palms and fingers (7 palms = 1 cubit, 4 fingers = 1 palm). All intermediate values are integer ratios.
systems.qa_equivalent: Seked = cotangent of slope angle. spread(slope) = rise²/(rise²+run²) = den²/(den²+num²). This IS a QA quadrance ratio. Egyptian fractions (cert [134]) = same arithmetic.

seked_witnesses.label: Great Pyramid of Giza (seked 5½ per 7)
seked_witnesses._note: seked_num=11 (run), seked_den=2 (rise). spread = 2²/(2²+11²) = 4/125 = 0.032 exactly. This represents the reciprocal reading: 11 units horizontal per 2 units vertical.

seked_witnesses.label: Great Pyramid CORRECTED (rise=14, run=11)
seked_witnesses._note: Seked = 11 run per 14 rise. spread = 14²/(14²+11²) = 196/317 = 0.6183. This matches slope angle 51.84°.

systems.name: Polynesian Star Compass
systems.method: 32 named star houses around the horizon. Each house = a star's rising or setting azimuth. Dead reckoning by 'etak' (moving reference island). Memorized star paths = discrete lookup table.
systems.qa_equivalent: Mod-32 discrete bearing system. Each star house = a QA direction. The etak system = T-operator dead reckoning with reference frame shift. 32 houses ≈ mod-24 (both prime-power multiples of 8). Entirely discrete, no continuous computation.
systems._note: The Polynesian navigator computes position by counting star houses and island-crossing times — integer arithmetic on a discrete lattice. This IS QA navigation.

systems.name: Norse Sun Stones
systems.method: Iceland spar (calcite) crystal depolarizes skylight to locate sun through clouds. Observer rotates crystal until polarization pattern indicates sun direction. Combined with 'sun shadow board' for latitude measurement at noon.
systems.qa_equivalent: The calcite crystal measures the SPREAD of polarization angle — sin²(θ_polarization). This is a direct spread measurement instrument, like the sextant. Combined with latitude sailing (constant-latitude loxodrome = cert [166]), Norse navigation = spread measurement + discrete loxodrome.
systems._note: Ropars et al. (2012) confirmed experimentally that calcite sun stones can locate the sun to within 1° through overcast. The measurement is inherently a spread (polarization intensity ratio).

systems.name: Arab Kamal
systems.method: Rectangular wooden card on a knotted string. Card held at arm's length; knots mark fixed string lengths. Star altitude measured in finger-widths (isba). Integer increments only — no fractional readings.
systems.qa_equivalent: Each finger-width ≈ 1.5° ≈ a fixed spread increment. Altitude in fingers → spread via s = sin²(f × 1.5°). The kamal IS a discrete spread-measuring instrument. Latitude determination = matching observed spread to tabulated port spreads.

kamal_witnesses.label: 20 fingers (30°)
kamal_witnesses._note: s = sin²(30°) = 1/4 exactly. A QA-nice value.

kamal_witnesses.label: 34 fingers (51°)
kamal_witnesses._note: ~latitude of London. 34 finger-widths on the kamal.

qa_connection.common_structure: All 5 systems share: (a) discrete direction states, (b) integer arithmetic, (c) observer projection only at measurement boundaries. This is Theorem NT in practice, independently discovered across 5 civilizations and 4000 years.
qa_connection.convergence: Babylon (direction table) + Egypt (slope ratio) + Polynesia (star compass) + Norse (polarization spread) + Arabia (finger-width spread) — each is a partial realization of the same QA navigation architecture. None had the complete framework; each discovered the piece relevant to their geography and technology.
qa_connection.modern_regression: The Portuguese/European adoption of continuous trigonometry (15th-16th century) was a REGRESSION from the integer-ratio methods. It introduced the very float errors that QA eliminates. The history of navigation is: integer (ancient) → continuous (modern) → integer (QA).

sources: Plimpton 322: Mansfield & Wildberger, Historia Mathematica (2017); Math Intelligencer (2021)
Egyptian seked: Gillings, Mathematics in the Time of the Pharaohs (1972)
Polynesian navigation: Lewis, We the Navigators (1972); Gladwin, East is a Big Bird (1970)
Norse sun stones: Ropars et al., Proc. Roy. Soc. A (2012)
Arab kamal: Tibbetts, Arab Navigation in the Indian Ocean Before the Coming of the Portuguese (1971)
